behavior_summary: Provides examine (U8P_GRIMOIRE::look) and U8P_GRIMOIRE::use behavior for a grimoire-type item. The U8P_GRIMOIRE::look() process maps the item's numeric quality code (Item::getQuality(this)) to a human-readable book title and displays it when the player inspects the item.

The U8P_GRIMOIRE::use() helper prepares the item for reading: it appears to award an intelligence increment (AccumulateIntelligence(0x03)), stores a reference to the item in a local (local1 = this), and spawns a book-specific reader routine (several spawn addressof(local1)->BOOKX::func0080 variants) to present the book's contents, then suspends until that reader executes/returns.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GRIMOIRE::look implements a switch-like conditional on Item::getQuality(this) and issues a matching display (Bark) for each quality code (i.e., maps quality IDs to book titles).

U8P_GRIMOIRE::use does a linear sequence: assign local1=this, call AccumulateIntelligence(0x03) (inferred), then branch on Item::getQuality(this) to spawn the appropriate reader process and suspend execution; spawn result is captured in a temp local.
No global flags are read or written by either routine in the available excerpt.
open_questions: Exact mapping from quality IDs to reader routines and the full list of BOOKx reader targets (metadata shows many spawn variants but mapping completeness is uncertain).

Whether AccumulateIntelligence(0x03) always triggers on U8P_GRIMOIRE::use() or only for certain quality IDs—call pattern suggests it is unconditional but this is inferred from low-confidence snippets.
Precise behavior and UI flow of the spawned BOOK1/STEBOOK/EARTHMAG routines (do they show pages, advance skills, or just display flavor text?).
Whether any global flags outside this excerpt are set by the reader routines (reading side effects are outside this file).

main_function.name: U8P_GRIMOIRE::look
main_function.purpose: When the player inspects the grimoire item, read its quality code and display the corresponding book/spellbook title (short description).
entry_conditions: Triggered by the game's examine/U8P_GRIMOIRE::look action on the item (process type 0x0208).
Runs whenever the player 'looks at' the item (no special global flags required).

function_map.name: U8P_GRIMOIRE::look
function_map.role: Display the short description (title) for the grimoire based on its quality code.

function_map.name: U8P_GRIMOIRE::use
function_map.role: Handle using/reading the grimoire: award intelligence (inferred), set up local reference to the item, spawn the book reader process appropriate to the quality code, then suspend.

function_map.name: Item::getQuality
function_map.role: Provide the quality/ID integer for this Item instance; drives title selection and reader dispatch.

function_map.name: AccumulateIntelligence
function_map.role: Increment or award intelligence/knowledge when the book is used (inferred call with constant 0x03).

function_map.name: spawn addressof(local1)->BOOK1::func0080 (and variants)
function_map.role: Spawn/invoke book-specific reader/display processes for the item instance passed as local1.

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the numeric quality code for the item to select which title/bark to display.
Show the chosen human-readable title or short description to the player when examined.
Award/increment intelligence or knowledge on item U8P_GRIMOIRE::use (observed call with constant 0x03; inferred learning reward).
Check the item's quality code to decide which book reader process to spawn.

external_calls.callee: spawn addressof(local1)->BOOK1::func0080 (and other BOOKx variants like EARTHMAG::func0080, STEVBOOK::func0080)
external_calls.purpose: Launch the specific reader/handler for the chosen book instance, passing the item reference (local1).
Pause the using process while the spawned reader executes or until control returns.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides examine (U8P_GRIMOIRE::look) and U8P_GRIMOIRE::use behavior for a grimoire-type item. The U8P_GRIMOIRE::look() process maps the item's numeric quality code (Item::getQuality(this)) to a human-readable book title and displays it when the player inspects the item.
